target.name: DEFCON AI
target.founder: Yisroel Brumer, Paul Selva, Grant Verstandig

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DEFCON AI is a Seed-stage defense and security AI company founded by Yisroel Brumer, Paul Selva, and Grant Verstandig. The company has raised $44M to date. The Defense & Security sector includes 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, with notable comparables including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The valuation analysis applies Stage-Progression methodology, which is the applicable triangulation method given data constraints. Using $44M in total capital raised as a proxy for enterprise value at Seed stage and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $110M. A Peer-Median approach was not feasible; while 19 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three comparables met narrow filters for stage, timing, and valuation tier, making a median-based estimate unreliable without introducing sector-wide bias.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $94M to $126M, with a median point of $110M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single methodology and the absence of narrow-set peer validation. Key sensitivities include: (1) the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific stage transition in defense tech, and (2) the degree to which DEFCON AI's technology differentiation and team track record command a premium or discount relative to the broader peer set. Additional revenue, customer traction, or proprietary technology data would materially refine this range.
